title: 1.1888 BPB via SP-4096 compression + stride-64 sliding window
body:
## Summary
- **val_bpb: 1.1888** (post-quant, sliding window eval stride=64)
- SP-4096 tokenizer (0.30 tokens/byte vs 0.41) + stride-64 sliding window eval — two independent improvements that stack multiplicatively via BPB formula
- 8-layer 512-dim GQA encoder-decoder with skip connections, Muon optimizer
- Artifact: 15.68MB (under 16MB), 14,006 steps in 600s on 8xH100

## Key innovations
1. **SP-4096 tokenizer**: 26.6% better compression ratio (3.32 bytes/token vs 2.44), directly reduces the tokens/byte multiplier in BPB
2. **Sliding window evaluation**: Overlapping stride-64 windows give each token ~960 context tokens instead of ~512 average, lowering cross-entropy

## Results
| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| val_bpb (post-quant, sliding) | **1.1888** |
| val_bpb (pre-quant) | 1.2067 |
| val_loss (nats) | 2.7351 |
| Steps | 14,006 |
| Wallclock | 600s |
| Artifact size | 15.68MB |

## Test plan
- [x] Post-quant val_bpb verified via int8+zlib roundtrip
- [x] Artifact under 16MB
- [x] Runs within 600s wallclock on 8xH100
- [x] train_gpt.py compiles and runs from records folder
